0.original: In one of my first jobs, I worked closely with another young fellow,
0.b1: In one of my first jobs, I worked closely with a young man.
0.cn: 在我早期的工作中，我曾与一名年轻人密切共事。
0.ai_notes: 'young fellow': a friendly or informal way to refer to a young man.
0.remarks: 'young fellow': 年轻人，一种非正式且友好的称呼。

1.original: whom I'll call F.H. F.H. was like many persons you know.
1.b1: I will call him F.H. He was like many people you know.
1.cn: 我称他为F.H.。他就像你认识的许多人一样。
1.ai_notes: 'whom': a formal word used to refer to the object of a verb or preposition.
1.remarks: 'whom': 谁，用于指代动词或介词的宾语，语气较为正式。

2.original: He was preoccupied with why he needed more money instead of being preoccupied with ways to make money.
2.b1: He worried about needing more money instead of thinking about how to earn it.
2.cn: 他总是纠结于自己需要更多钱，而不是思考如何去赚钱。
2.ai_notes: 'preoccupied with': thinking about something so much that you do not pay attention to other things.
2.remarks: 'preoccupied with': 全神贯注于某事，以至于忽略了其他事情。

3.original: Each week, F.H. spent hours of company time working on his personal budget problems.
3.b1: Every week, F.H. spent hours at work trying to solve his personal money problems.
3.cn: 每周，F.H.都会花几个小时的工作时间来处理他个人的财务问题。
3.ai_notes: 'company time': the hours you are paid to work for your employer.
3.remarks: 'company time': 工作时间，指雇主支付薪水让你工作的时段。

4.original: His favorite topic of conversation was, I'm the most underpaid man here, let me tell you why.
4.b1: He loved to say, "I am underpaid, let me tell you why."
4.cn: 他最喜欢谈论的话题就是：“我的薪水太低了，让我告诉你为什么。”
4.ai_notes: 'underpaid': receiving less money than your work is actually worth.
4.remarks: 'underpaid': 薪水过低，指获得的报酬低于工作实际价值。

5.original: F.H. had the not uncommon attitude of, this is a big company,
5.b1: F.H. had a common attitude: this is a big company,
5.cn: F.H.有一种很常见的想法：这是一家大公司，
5.ai_notes: 'not uncommon': a way of saying something is actually quite common or frequent.
5.remarks: 'not uncommon': 并不罕见，委婉地表示某事其实很常见。

6.original: it's netting millions, it's paying a lot of people big salaries,
6.b1: it makes millions and pays many people high salaries,
6.cn: 它赚取数百万美元，给很多人发高薪，
6.ai_notes: 'netting': earning a profit after all expenses are paid.
6.remarks: 'netting': 净赚，指扣除所有开支后获得的利润。

7.original: so it ought to pay me more, too.
7.b1: so it should pay me more too.
7.cn: 所以它也应该多付我一些钱。
7.ai_notes: 'ought to': a way to say that something is the right or expected thing to do.
7.remarks: 'ought to': 应该，表示做某事是正确或预期的行为。

8.original: F.H. had been passed over several times for pay increases.
8.b1: F.H. had been denied pay raises several times.
8.ai_notes: 'passed over': ignored or not chosen for something, like a promotion or raise.
8.remarks: 'passed over': 被忽略，指未被选中获得晋升或加薪等机会。

9.original: Finally, one day he decided that it was high time he went in there and demanded more money.
9.b1: Finally, he decided it was time to ask for more money.
9.cn: 终于有一天，他决定是时候去要求涨工资了。
9.ai_notes: 'high time': a phrase meaning it is past the time when something should have happened.
9.remarks: 'high time': 早该……了，表示某事早就应该发生却拖延至今。

10.original: About thirty minutes later, F.H. was back, all heated up.
10.b1: Thirty minutes later, F.H. returned, very angry.
10.cn: 大约三十分钟后，F.H.回来了，非常愤怒。
10.ai_notes: 'all heated up': very angry or upset.
10.remarks: 'all heated up': 非常生气或心烦意乱。

11.original: His expression made it obvious that next month's check would look exactly like this month's check.
11.b1: His face showed that his next paycheck would not increase.
11.cn: 他的表情说明下个月的工资单和这个月的一模一样。
11.ai_notes: 'expression': the look on someone's face that shows how they are feeling.
11.remarks: 'expression': 表情，指脸上流露出的情绪状态。

12.original: Immediately, F.H. began to let go.
12.ai_notes: 'let go': in this context, it means to release one's anger by complaining.
12.remarks: 'let go': 在此语境下指通过抱怨来宣泄愤怒。

13.ai_notes: 'Boy': an exclamation used to express strong emotion, like surprise or anger.
13.remarks: 'Boy': 天哪，用作感叹词，表达惊讶或愤怒等强烈情绪。

14.original: What do you suppose the old man said when I told him I wanted more money?
14.b1: What do you think the boss said when I asked for more money?
14.cn: 当我告诉老板我想加薪时，你猜他说了什么？
14.ai_notes: 'suppose': to think or believe something might be true.
14.remarks: 'suppose': 认为，推测某事可能是真的。

15.original: He had the gall to ask me, Why do you believe you are justified in asking for an increase?
15.b1: He actually asked me, "Why do you think you deserve a raise?"
15.cn: 他竟然厚着脸皮问我：“你凭什么认为自己有资格要求加薪？”
15.ai_notes: 'have the gall': to be rude or bold enough to do something shocking; 'justified': having a good or fair reason for doing something.
15.remarks:
'have the gall': 厚颜无耻，指有胆量做令人震惊的无礼之事。
'justified': 合理的，指做某事有正当理由。

16.original: I gave him plenty of reasons, F.H. went on.
16.b1: I gave him many reasons, F.H. continued.
16.cn: 我给了他很多理由，F.H.继续说道。

17.original: I told him I'd been passed over when others around here were getting pay raises.
17.b1: I told him I was ignored while others got raises.
17.cn: 我告诉他，当别人加薪时，我却被忽略了。
17.ai_notes: 'passed over': not selected for a reward or opportunity that others received.
17.remarks: 'passed over': 被冷落，指未获得他人应得的奖励或机会。

18.original: I told him my bills are getting larger and my paycheck isn't.
18.b1: I told him my bills are rising but my pay is not.
18.cn: 我告诉他，我的账单越来越多，但工资却没涨。
18.ai_notes: 'paycheck': the amount of money you earn from your job.
18.remarks: 'paycheck': 薪水，指从工作中获得的报酬金额。

19.original: And I told him that I do everything around here they ask me to do.
19.b1: And I told him I do everything they ask me to do.
19.cn: 我还告诉他，公司要求我做的事，我全都做了。
19.ai_notes: 'do everything around here': to perform all the tasks assigned to you.
19.remarks: 'do everything around here': 包揽一切，指承担了分配给自己的所有任务。

20.ai_notes: 'Can you beat that?': an expression of disbelief or shock at something unfair or surprising.
20.remarks: 'Can you beat that?': 还有比这更离谱的吗？用于表达对不公或意外之事的震惊。

21.original: I need a raise, but instead of paying me more, they give out raises to other guys around here who don't need it half as much as I do.
21.b1: I need a raise, but they give it to others who don't need it as much as I do.
21.cn: 我需要加薪，但他们却把钱给了那些并不像我这么需要钱的人。
21.ai_notes: 'give out': to distribute or provide things to other people.
21.remarks: 'give out': 分发，指提供东西给他人。

22.original: Why, the way he acted, F.H. continued, you'd think I was asking for charity.
22.b1: F.H. added, he acted like I was asking for charity.
22.cn: F.H.继续说，他那态度，简直就像我在乞讨一样。
22.ai_notes: 'charity': help or money given to people who are in need.
22.remarks: 'charity': 慈善，指给予有需要的人帮助或金钱。

23.original: All he would say is, when your record shows that you deserve more money, you'll get more money.
23.b1: He only said, "When your work shows you deserve more money, you will get it."
23.cn: 他只说了一句：“当你的工作表现证明你值得更多薪水时，你自然会得到。”
23.ai_notes: 'record': the history of your past work performance and achievements.
23.remarks: 'record': 记录，指过去的工作表现和成就历史。

24.original: Sure, I could do a better job if they paid me for it,
24.b1: Sure, I could work better if they paid me more,
24.cn: 当然，如果他们给我加薪，我工作会更努力，
24.ai_notes: 'Sure': a casual way to say 'certainly' or 'I agree'.
24.remarks: 'Sure': 当然，一种随意的表示同意或肯定的方式。

25.original: but only a fool does something he isn't paid for.
25.b1: but only a fool works hard without being paid for it.
25.cn: 但只有傻瓜才会做没有报酬的工作。
25.ai_notes: 'fool': a person who lacks good sense or judgment.
25.remarks: 'fool': 傻瓜，指缺乏常识或判断力的人。

26.original: F.H. is an example of the breed that is blind to the how of making money.
26.b1: F.H. is an example of someone who doesn't understand how to make money.
26.cn: F.H.就是那种不懂赚钱之道的人的典型。
26.ai_notes: 'breed': a group of people with similar characteristics; 'blind to': unable to see or understand something.
26.remarks:
'breed': 一类人，指具有相似特征的群体。
'blind to': 对……视而不见，指无法看到或理解某事。

27.original: His last remark sums up his mistake.
27.b1: His last comment shows his mistake.
27.cn: 他最后那句话总结了他的错误。
27.ai_notes: 'sums up': to state the main points or the final conclusion of something.
27.remarks: 'sums up': 总结，指陈述要点或得出最终结论。

28.original: In effect, FH wanted the company to pay him more and then he would produce more.
28.b1: F.H. wanted the company to pay him more before he worked harder.
28.cn: 实际上，F.H.希望公司先给他加薪，然后他才愿意多做工作。
28.ai_notes: 'in effect': basically or for all practical purposes.
28.remarks: 'in effect': 实际上，指从实际效果来看。

29.original: But this is not how the system works.
29.b1: But that is not how the system works.
29.cn: 但这并不是职场的运作方式。
29.ai_notes: 'the system': the way things are organized or how a business operates.
29.remarks: 'the system': 体制，指事物的组织方式或商业运作模式。

30.original: You don't get a raise on the promise of better performance.
30.b1: You don't get a raise just by promising to do better.
30.cn: 你不能靠承诺表现更好来获得加薪。
30.ai_notes: 'promise': a statement that you will definitely do something in the future.
30.remarks: 'promise': 承诺，指明确表示未来一定会做某事。

31.original: You get a raise only by demonstrating better performance.
31.b1: You get a raise only by showing better performance.
31.cn: 你只能通过展现出更好的工作成果来获得加薪。
31.ai_notes: 'demonstrating': showing clearly through actions or evidence.
31.remarks: 'demonstrating': 展示，通过行动或证据清楚地表现出来。

32.original: You can't harvest money unless you plant the seeds that grow money.
32.b1: You cannot harvest money if you do not plant the seeds first.
32.cn: 如果你不先播下种子，就无法收获金钱。
32.ai_notes: 'harvest': to collect crops after they have grown; 'plant the seeds': to start the process that leads to a result.
32.remarks:
'harvest': 收获，指作物成熟后的收割。
'plant the seeds': 播种，比喻开启导致某种结果的过程。

33.original: And the seed of money is service.
33.b1: And the seed of money is service.
33.cn: 而金钱的种子就是服务。
33.ai_notes: 'service': the act of helping others or doing work that provides value.
33.remarks: 'service': 服务，指帮助他人或提供有价值的工作。

34.original: Put service first and money takes care of itself.
34.b1: Focus on your service first, and the money will follow.
34.cn: 把服务放在第一位，金钱自然会随之而来。
34.ai_notes: 'takes care of itself': happens naturally without needing extra effort.
34.remarks: 'takes care of itself': 自然解决，指无需额外努力就能顺利进行。
